title: impl: adapt SS14 grafana dashboards for VS14
description:
## Context
SS14 community provides two Grafana dashboards as JSON exports:
- Game Servers (uid K0LTdKmMk): fleet overview
- Perf Metrics (uid qyFqpFiGk): per-server deep dive with Logs panel

Source: docs.spacestation14.com/en/community/infrastructure-reference/grafana-dashboards.html

## Dependencies
- vs-2p3 (prometheus/loki/grafana stack) must be running

## Task
Adapt the upstream SS14 dashboards for Vacation Station. Store JSON in repo.
Provision automatically on Grafana startup via provisioning.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Download and adapt
- [ ] Fetch raw dashboard JSON from docs page
- [ ] Store at ops/grafana/dashboards/game-servers.json
- [ ] Store at ops/grafana/dashboards/perf-metrics.json

### Adaptations
- [ ] Replace datasource UIDs with our Grafana's
- [ ] Verify `job="gameservers"` matches our Prometheus scrape config
- [ ] Verify `server` label values match (our instance name)
- [ ] Rename where appropriate (e.g. "Wizden Servers" -> "Vacation Station")

### Provisioning
- [ ] ops/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml
- [ ] ops/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml with file provider
  pointing at /var/lib/grafana/dashboards/
- [ ] docker-compose mounts dashboards + provisioning into Grafana container
- [ ] On fresh Grafana startup, both dashboards auto-appear

### Verification
- [ ] Game Servers dashboard shows our instance
- [ ] Perf Metrics dashboard's $Server variable populated with our instance name
- [ ] Logs panel on Perf Metrics shows live Loki logs
- [ ] Connections panel works (requires Postgres datasource configured)

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md dashboard section
- [ ] Customization notes (where to edit for fork-specific metrics)
- [ ] How to add new panels or dashboards

title: harness: wire hooks to .claude/settings.json
description:
## Context
Hooks are in hooks/ but not registered. Need .claude/settings.json so Claude
Code picks them up automatically in this repo.

## Task
Create .claude/settings.json using $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to reference hooks/*.sh
so they work from any session opened in this repo.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .claude/settings.json created with all 9 hook registrations
- [ ] Paths use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (works from any session)
- [ ] Verified hooks directory is still executable

title: harness: CLAUDE.md and foundation files

create _VS directory structure, keep FORK_POINT.

title: ops: add services-flake dev stack (postgres + observability)
description:
## Context
Production bring-up of postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana is handled by the ops scripts + docker-compose we shipped (vs-3ty/vs-h3u/vs-2p3). That path is correct for the Ubuntu host. For local dev we want a zero-sudo, ephemeral equivalent so contributors can validate config changes without touching docker or a real host.

services-flake (https://community.flake.parts/services-flake) is a flake-parts module set that wraps process-compose to run a stack of services as user processes with data under a project-local dir.

Goal: `nix run .#dev-services` boots postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana on localhost against the SAME configs we committed in `ops/`, with data in `.data/` (gitignored).

## Dependencies
- vs-2ti (ops validation tools in dev shell) — not strictly blocking, but land that first so `promtool` etc. are available to validate configs the dev stack loads.

## Task
Extend `flake.nix` to add a services-flake output that composes:
- `services.postgres.pg1` — port 5432, database `vacation_station`, user `vs14`, password `dev-only-insecure` (literal — this is dev-only, document that)
- `services.prometheus.prom1` — config from `ops/observability/prometheus.yml` (or a dev-overlay that points the scrape target at the dev SS14 server port on localhost)
- `services.loki.loki1` — config from `ops/observability/loki-config.yml` (or dev-overlay as needed)
- `services.grafana.graf1` — admin password `admin` (dev only), provisioning dir pointing at `ops/observability/grafana/provisioning`, dashboards dir at `ops/observability/grafana/dashboards`

## Scope
1. **`flake.nix` inputs**: add `services-flake` (from `github:juspay/services-flake`) and `process-compose-flake` (`github:platonic-systems/process-compose-flake`).
2. **`flake.nix` outputs**: switch from `flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem` to `flake-parts.lib.mkFlake` (services-flake requires flake-parts). Preserve the existing `devShells.default = import ./shell.nix { inherit pkgs; }` output — don't break the dev shell that's already working.
3. **New flake output**: `packages.<system>.dev-services` (or `apps.<system>.dev-services`) — the process-compose entrypoint.
4. **Service configs**:
   - Postgres: `initialDatabases = [{ name = "vacation_station"; }]` to match prod schema
   - Prometheus: load our committed `ops/observability/prometheus.yml`. If the `scrape_configs` target `host.docker.internal:44880` needs a dev override (docker-specific hostname won't resolve in dev), put a small nix-side overlay that rewrites the target to `localhost:44880`. Document this.
   - Loki: load `ops/observability/loki-config.yml`. If the `path_prefix` needs to be rewritten to a dev-local dir, do it in the nix expression.
   - Grafana: provisioning + dashboards from the committed paths. Datasource URLs point at the dev-stack services.
5. **Data persistence**: all services write state to `.data/{postgres,prometheus,loki,grafana}/` (project-relative). Update root `.gitignore` to ignore `.data/`.
6. **Docs**: add a "Dev Stack" subsection to `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` (create if absent) covering:
   - `nix run .#dev-services` to start
   - How to view logs (process-compose TUI)
   - How to reset (`rm -rf .data/`)
   - Credentials (dev-only literals; never reuse for prod)
   - Relationship to production (same configs, same schema, different storage)
   - SS14 connection: point game server's `config.toml` `[database]` at `localhost:5432` user `vs14` pw `dev-only-insecure`; `[metrics]` and `[loki]` already point at localhost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `nix flake check` passes (or at minimum: `nix-instantiate --parse flake.nix` succeeds and `nix flake show` lists the new output)
- [ ] `nix run .#dev-services --help` (or equivalent) resolves — you don't need to actually boot the stack in the worktree, just prove the expression evaluates
- [ ] Existing `devShells.default` still evaluates (preserve `nix develop` behavior)
- [ ] `.data/` gitignored; no committed state files
- [ ] Dev-only credentials clearly commented as such in flake.nix and docs
- [ ] docs/DEVELOPMENT.md section documents bring-up, reset, credentials, schema-parity claim, and point back to vs-3ty/vs-2p3 for prod
- [ ] Single commit, `Bead: vs-1bb` trailer, gitmoji `:sparkles:`

## Out of scope
- Do NOT modify the production setup scripts or docker-compose
- Do NOT add SS14.Watchdog as a services-flake service (it's a single supervisor binary, not a service we'd want in dev alongside the stack — contributors run SS14 directly via `dotnet run` in dev)
- Do NOT attempt to actually boot the stack inside the worktree (process-compose wants a TTY and the services want ports; just prove the expression evaluates)

## Environment note
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` to nix commands so the flake toolchain is active. See CLAUDE.md "Nix env in worktree subagents".

## References to consult
- services-flake quickstart: https://community.flake.parts/services-flake
- Available services: https://community.flake.parts/services-flake/services
- flake-parts: https://flake.parts

## Commit
Example message:
```
:sparkles: ops: nix services-flake dev stack for postgres + observability

- flake.nix: add services-flake + process-compose-flake inputs, migrate to flake-parts
- packages.<system>.dev-services: postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana,
  data in ./.data/ (gitignored), binds to localhost, zero sudo required
- Services read the same configs committed in ops/ (with small dev overlays
  for host.docker.internal -> localhost and data paths)
- docs/DEVELOPMENT.md: Dev Stack section (bring-up, reset, credentials,
  schema parity with prod, how to wire a dev SS14 instance against it)
- devShells.default preserved unchanged
- .gitignore: .data/

Dev-only credentials embedded (documented as such). Production path is
unchanged (vs-3ty postgres + vs-2p3 docker-compose stack remain canonical).

Bead: vs-1bb
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

title: human: run vs-h3u watchdog setup on host
description:
## Context
vs-h3u shipped setup.watchdog.sh, systemd unit, appsettings.yml.example, instance-bootstrap.sh, and extended config.toml.example + docs/OPERATIONS.md. None of it has run on the host.

## Dependencies
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup) complete — watchdog unit has `After=postgresql.service`

## Task — run on the target host
1. `sudo ./setup.watchdog.sh` (optionally `WATCHDOG_REF=<tag-or-sha> sudo -E ./setup.watchdog.sh` to pin upstream).
   - Creates system user `ss14:ss14`
   - Publishes SS14.Watchdog to `/opt/ss14-watchdog/`
   - Installs `ss14-watchdog.service`, enables (not starts)
   - Generates ApiToken into a fresh `appsettings.yml` (only if absent)
2. Capture the generated ApiToken from `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml`. Store in password manager under "Vacation Station / watchdog / vacation-station ApiToken". Never commit.
3. `sudo ./ops/watchdog/instance-bootstrap.sh` — sets up `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/` layout and seeds `config.toml` from the template.
4. Edit `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml`: fill in postgres password from password manager; adjust hub/auth/status tags as desired.
5. Drop a Robust.Server build into `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/binaries/` (Local UpdateType needs this for first launch). Until we publish builds, a `dotnet publish` of our content + engine works.
6. Start the service: `sudo systemctl start ss14-watchdog`.
7. Verify:
   - `systemctl is-active ss14-watchdog` → active
   - `pstree -p $(pgrep -f SS14.Watchdog)` → shows `Robust.Server` child
   - `curl -sS http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/ping` responds (exact path per watchdog version)
   - API auth: `curl -u vacation-station:$TOKEN -X POST http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/restart` succeeds
   - Crash recovery: `sudo pkill -f Robust.Server`; confirm watchdog respawns it within TimeoutSeconds (default 60)
8. Firewall:
   - Confirm port 5000 NOT public (`ss -tln | grep :5000` shows 127.0.0.1 only; cloud firewall closed)
   - Confirm UDP 1212 + TCP 1212 open inbound per docs/NETWORKING.md (game server status + main port)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `systemctl is-active ss14-watchdog` → active
- [ ] Game server running as child of watchdog (pstree)
- [ ] ApiToken stored in password manager
- [ ] API ping + auth'd restart both succeed from localhost
- [ ] Crash recovery verified (kill Robust.Server, watchdog respawns)
- [ ] Port 5000 localhost-only; 1212 open per networking doc
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp in this bead before closing

## Notes
- Loki sink in appsettings.yml is intentionally commented out (waits on vs-2p3). Don't enable yet.
- UpdateType remains `Local` until we have a Manifest CDN. Don't change without planning an update pipeline.
- Report any divergence from setup.watchdog.sh so we can PR fixes back.

title: docs: networking guide with caddy and firewall setup
description:
## Context
User is hosting on a headless VPS. Needs documentation for accessing the server
from outside, firewall considerations (ufw), and adding a domain name via Caddy.

## Task
Write docs/NETWORKING.md covering:
- App binding vs firewall (two layers)
- ufw basics and setup
- Cloud provider firewall
- Domain name setup (DNS, config)
- Caddy reverse proxy for HTTPS on status API
- UDP can't be proxied (kept direct)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear explanation of binding vs firewall
- [ ] Caddy config example
- [ ] Firewall rules for SS14 server
- [ ] Testing procedure from external machine

title: human: verify vs-13x dashboards load in grafana
description:
## Context
vs-13x shipped two adapted Grafana dashboards (game-servers.json, perf-metrics.json) into ops/observability/grafana/dashboards/ plus docs. Grafana's file provider will pick them up automatically on container start, but nobody has actually confirmed they render correctly against live data.

## Dependencies
- vs-25k (observability stack running) complete — Grafana must be up and datasources green

## Task — verify on the host with a running stack
1. Ensure grafana container has the latest dashboards: `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml restart grafana` (the file provider rescans on boot).
2. In Grafana UI → Dashboards: confirm both appear:
   - "Vacation Station Servers" (uid `vs14-game-servers`)
   - "Vacation Station Perf Metrics" (uid `vs14-perf-metrics`)
3. On **Game Servers**:
   - All panels render without the red "Datasource not found" banner
   - Player-count / round-length / server-list panels populate once SS14 is running
   - Connections panel (Postgres) shows at least 1 row (the grafana session itself counts)
4. On **Perf Metrics**:
   - The `$Server` dropdown populates with `vacation-station` from `label_values(ss14_round_length{job="gameservers"}, server)`
   - Logs panel streams live Loki logs: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}`
   - Perf panels (tick rate, process metrics) populate
5. Known TODOs to either resolve or backlog:
   - **TPS alert notifier** — upstream references notification UID `N5nihcmMk` that doesn't exist locally. Grafana logs "missing notifier"; alert still fires internally but can't deliver. Either create a local contact point + rebind, or delete the alert rule until alerting scope is formally opened.
   - **Stale `byName` overrides** for wizards_den_eu_west / us_west — harmless no-ops on our single-instance deployment; prune at your leisure via UI edit + export.
   - **Datasource uid fragility** — dashboards bind to datasource NAMES (`Prometheus`, `Loki`, `Postgres`). If someone later adds explicit `uid:` fields to datasources.yml, the dashboards will silently unbind. Mitigation: pin datasource uids to lowercase names in datasources.yml AND rewrite the dashboard JSONs to match.
6. If anything breaks, file a follow-up `fix:` bead referencing vs-13x and the specific panel/UID at fault.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both dashboards visible under Dashboards in Grafana UI with the pinned UIDs
- [ ] All panels on both dashboards render without datasource errors
- [ ] `$Server` variable on Perf Metrics populates from live Prometheus labels
- [ ] Logs panel on Perf Metrics streams live log lines from the running SS14 server
- [ ] Decision recorded on the TPS alert notifier TODO (local contact point or removed)
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp before closing

## Notes
- A Robust.Server must be actively running for most panels to show data. First-boot empty panels are expected; re-check after a round has started.
- Dashboards are provisioned read-only. To iterate, "Save As..." a copy, then export + replace the committed JSON, then `docker compose restart grafana`.

title: human: bring up vs-2p3 observability stack on host
description:
## Context
vs-2p3 shipped docker-compose + prometheus/loki/grafana configs + SS14/watchdog wiring + ops docs. Nothing is running on the host. This bead captures the host-only bring-up.

## Dependencies
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup) complete — Grafana Postgres datasource expects it
- vs-1np (watchdog host setup) complete — Loki receives watchdog + game server logs
- Docker Engine installed on the host (if not yet: `curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh` or per distro)

## Task — run on the target host
1. **Bootstrap secrets** (NONE committed):
   - `mkdir -p ops/observability/secrets`
   - `openssl rand -base64 32 > ops/observability/secrets/grafana_admin_password.txt`
   - `cp ops/observability/.env.example ops/observability/.env`
   - Edit `ops/observability/.env`: set `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=` to the vs14 Postgres password from password manager
   - Confirm both files gitignored (`.gitignore` in ops/observability/ handles this — verify)
2. **Bring up the stack**:
   - `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml up -d`
   - `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml ps` — all three services healthy
3. **Verify endpoints** (all localhost):
   - `curl -sS localhost:9090/api/v1/targets | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | {job: .labels.job, health}'` — `gameservers` target reports `up` once SS14 is running
   - `curl -sS localhost:3100/ready` → `ready`
   - `curl -sS localhost:3000/api/health` → `{"database":"ok"}`
4. **Grafana login**:
   - Browse to `http://localhost:3000` (port-forward via SSH if remote)
   - Login as `admin` with password from `grafana_admin_password.txt`
   - Change admin password on first login (Grafana forces this)
   - Store the final admin password in password manager under "Vacation Station / grafana / admin"
5. **Datasource verification**:
   - Grafana → Connections → Data sources: Prometheus, Loki, PostgreSQL all auto-provisioned, all test-connection green
   - PostgreSQL datasource only works if `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` in `.env` is correct and Postgres `pg_hba.conf` permits host.docker.internal (127.0.0.1 usually fine)
6. **Metric + log sanity checks** (requires SS14 game server running):
   - In Grafana Explore with Prometheus: `robust_player_count{server="vacation-station"}` returns a series
   - In Grafana Explore with Loki: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}` returns log lines
   - Watchdog logs: `{Server="vacation-station-watchdog"}` or whatever Name you configured
7. **SS14 side wiring**:
   - Confirm `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml` has the `[metrics]` and `[loki]` blocks from config.toml.example
   - Confirm `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` has the `GrafanaLoki` Serilog sink enabled
   - Restart watchdog to pick up changes: `sudo systemctl restart ss14-watchdog`
8. **Firewall**:
   - `ss -tln | grep -E ':(9090|3100|3000)'` — all three bound to 127.0.0.1 only
   - Public Grafana via Caddy only (see docs/NETWORKING.md snippet)
9. **Caddy** (if external access desired):
   - Add the `grafana.yourdomain.com` block from docs/NETWORKING.md to your Caddyfile
   - `sudo systemctl reload caddy`
   - Verify HTTPS cert issued and proxy works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docker compose ps` shows prometheus + loki + grafana healthy
- [ ] `curl localhost:9090/api/v1/targets` shows `gameservers` target `up`
- [ ] `curl localhost:3100/ready` → `ready`
- [ ] Grafana admin password rotated on first login and stored in password manager
- [ ] All three datasources test-green in Grafana UI
- [ ] Prometheus + Loki sample queries return data (once SS14 is running)
- [ ] Ports 9090/3100/3000 localhost-only (confirmed with ss)
- [ ] Caddy HTTPS proxy functional (if external Grafana desired)
- [ ] Record completion timestamp before closing

## Notes
- Dashboards (vs-13x) land separately — this bead is stack bring-up only.
- If Prometheus `gameservers` target shows `down`, check that SS14 `[metrics] host = "localhost"` and the watchdog instance has actually started the game server on port 44880.
- Loki retention is 30d — raise by editing `ops/observability/loki-config.yml` `retention_period` and restarting the loki container.

title: human: run setup.ubuntu.sh --server on prod host
description:
## Context
All the other prod human beads (vs-y6o postgres, vs-1np watchdog, vs-25k observability, vs-205 dashboards) assume the host already has the baseline runtime: .NET 10 SDK + ASP.NET Core 10 runtime, git, python, build tools, firewall tooling. `setup.ubuntu.sh` is the committed script that does that. This bead is the bootstrap step they all depend on.

Nix is the primary DEV path (see docs/DEVELOPMENT.md and /nix skill), but production hosts run system-installed .NET via this script — not nix.

## Dependencies
- None (this is the root of the prod install tree)

## Blocks
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup)
- vs-1np (watchdog host setup) — specifically needs .NET 10 SDK to `dotnet publish` SS14.Watchdog from source
- vs-25k (observability stack bring-up) — docker is typically installed separately but `setup.ubuntu.sh --server` may or may not include it (verify)
- vs-205 (dashboard verification) — transitively via vs-25k

## Task — run on the target host

1. **Fresh-host sanity**: `lsb_release -a` should report Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 (what setup.ubuntu.sh targets). If not, confirm parity or switch host.
2. **Clone repo**: `git clone https://github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14.git /opt/vacation-station` (or wherever you want production repo root; this matches the path ops/watchdog/ss14-backup.service assumes).
3. **Run setup.ubuntu.sh**: `cd /opt/vacation-station && sudo ./setup.ubuntu.sh --server`.
   - `--server` flag omits the client-only graphics/audio deps (no X, no OpenGL, no PulseAudio). Keeps the host minimal.
   - If `--server` flag isn't implemented yet in the committed script, check what flags it takes and pick the server-minimal path; if only a single mode exists, run without flag and report what got installed.
   - Script should install: .NET 10 SDK, ASP.NET Core 10 runtime, git, python3, curl, build-essential.
4. **Verify .NET**: `dotnet --version` reports a 10.x SDK; `dotnet --list-runtimes` includes `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 10.x`.
5. **Verify python**: `python3 --version` ≥ 3.7 (for `RUN_THIS.py`).
6. **Initialize submodule**: `cd /opt/vacation-station && python3 RUN_THIS.py` — pulls RobustToolbox.
7. **Install Docker** (if not already, needed for vs-25k observability stack): `curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sudo sh` and `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER` (log out/in or `newgrp docker`). Verify: `docker compose version` → v2.x.
8. **Firewall baseline** (see docs/NETWORKING.md for full detail): `sudo ufw allow OpenSSH`, `sudo ufw enable`. Other per-service ports come from subsequent beads (1212 for SS14, etc.).
9. **Create ops user** (if setup.ubuntu.sh doesn't): `ss14` system user + group referenced by the watchdog systemd unit (vs-h3u script creates it but sanity-check here that `id ss14` works after its install).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 confirmed
- [ ] `/opt/vacation-station` (or chosen prod path) cloned, main up-to-date
- [ ] `setup.ubuntu.sh` ran cleanly (no error exit)
- [ ] `dotnet --version` ≥ 10.0.x
- [ ] `dotnet --list-runtimes` includes `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 10.x`
- [ ] `python3 RUN_THIS.py` completes; `RobustToolbox/` is populated
- [ ] `docker compose version` returns ≥ v2.x (install separately if setup.ubuntu.sh doesn't cover it)
- [ ] `ufw status` shows enabled with at least OpenSSH allowed
- [ ] Record completion timestamp + any setup.ubuntu.sh deviations in this bead before closing

## Notes
- setup.ubuntu.sh is *checked in* from upstream Delta-V; it may get updates via upstream-sync. Always pull before running.
- This bead is the **system-install bootstrap**. Contributors doing dev work should use nix/direnv instead (see docs/DEVELOPMENT.md Quick Start (Nix)).
- If anything in `setup.ubuntu.sh` fails or drifts from reality, file a `fix:` bead so we can PR corrections back (especially if deps change in future .NET / Ubuntu releases).
- This bead captures only baseline OS-level setup. Service-specific bring-up (postgres, watchdog, observability, dashboards) are in vs-y6o, vs-1np, vs-25k, vs-205 respectively — run them in that order after this one.

title: ops: prometheus + loki + grafana stack
description:
## Context
Level 5 observability stack: Prometheus for metrics, Loki for logs, Grafana for
visualization. SS14 server has native support for both (port 44880 exposes
Prometheus metrics, Serilog.Sinks.Loki pushes logs directly).

SS14.Watchdog also supports Loki push via its own Serilog config.

## Dependencies
- vs-h3u (watchdog) should be in progress or done (watchdog-level metrics/logs are valuable)
- vs-3ty (postgres) done (Grafana Connections panel queries Postgres)

## Task
Deploy Prometheus + Loki + Grafana via docker-compose on the same host as the
game server. Wire SS14 server and Watchdog to push metrics and logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

### docker-compose stack
- [ ] ops/docker-compose.yml in repo (or ops/observability/docker-compose.yml)
- [ ] Services: prometheus, loki, grafana (consider alertmanager later)
- [ ] Persistent volumes for each (survive container restart)
- [ ] Network isolation (stack internal, only Grafana exposed via Caddy)

### Prometheus
- [ ] ops/prometheus.yml scrape config:
  - job_name: "gameservers" (MUST match dashboard expectations)
  - Static target: host.docker.internal:44880 (or bridge network)
  - Labels: server: "vacation-station" (matches Loki name)
  - scrape_interval: 15s (docs say 1s, that's too aggressive)
- [ ] Prometheus UI reachable at localhost:9090
- [ ] Targets showing "UP" status
- [ ] Sample query `robust_player_count` returns data

### Loki
- [ ] ops/loki-config.yml with retention policy
- [ ] Loki reachable at localhost:3100
- [ ] Labels filter works: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}`
- [ ] Retention configured (e.g. 30 days)

### SS14 server config
- [ ] instances/vacation-station/config.toml:
  [metrics]
  enabled = true
  host = "localhost"
  port = 44880
  [loki]
  enabled = true
  address = "http://localhost:3100"
  name = "vacation-station"   # MUST match Prometheus `server` label

### SS14.Watchdog config
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml Serilog Loki sink:
  - Address: http://localhost:3100
  - Name: vacation-station-watchdog (or similar)

### Grafana
- [ ] Reachable at localhost:3000 (fronted by Caddy for HTTPS)
- [ ] Admin password from password manager (not default)
- [ ] Datasources configured:
  - Prometheus: http://prometheus:9090
  - Loki: http://loki:3100
  - PostgreSQL (grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin): Vacation Station DB
- [ ] grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin installed

### Firewall
- [ ] Prometheus, Loki internal-only (not on public ports)
- [ ] Grafana behind Caddy with HTTPS (docs/NETWORKING.md pattern)
- [ ] Caddy reverse proxy: grafana.yourdomain.com -> localhost:3000

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md observability section
- [ ] Troubleshooting: no metrics showing, no logs showing
- [ ] Instructions for adding alerts later

title: harness: enhance C# static analysis in hooks and /lint
setup: Ubuntu dev environment research & script
ops: extend nix dev shell with ops validation tools
description:
## Context
`shell.nix` currently ships dotnet/graphics/python deps for building the game. Ops scripts and subagents still `apt-get install shellcheck` etc. mid-run because the flake doesn't expose ops validation tools. Pin them in nix so everyone gets the same versions, and subagents can drop the ad-hoc apt calls.

## Task
Add ops validation tools to the `dependencies` list in `shell.nix`. Scope: tools that validate committed artifacts (prometheus/loki configs, shell scripts, yaml, systemd units).

## Scope — add to dependencies
- `shellcheck` — shell script linter (used by setup.postgres.sh, setup.watchdog.sh, backup.sh, etc.)
- `yamllint` — YAML linter (ops/observability/*.yml, docker-compose.yml, appsettings.yml.example)
- `prometheus` — provides `promtool` for `promtool check config ops/observability/prometheus.yml`
- `grafana-loki` or `loki` (whichever package name nixpkgs uses) — provides `loki -verify-config` and `logcli`
- `grafana` — provides `grafana-cli` for datasource/dashboard validation
- `systemd` — provides `systemd-analyze verify` (may already be present on the host; adding anyway for reproducibility; confirm it works inside `nix develop` on Linux)
- `go-task` / `just` — **skip** unless you already see one used; don't add speculatively

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `shell.nix` `dependencies` list extended; keep existing entries intact
- [ ] `nix-instantiate --parse shell.nix > /dev/null` succeeds (syntax)
- [ ] `nix develop -c which shellcheck promtool yamllint loki grafana-cli` all resolve to /nix/store paths (run only if you have confirmed nix daemon is available in the worktree env; otherwise document the validation path taken)
- [ ] `flake.lock` regenerated if changed (shell.nix change shouldn't bump it unless you touched flake.nix)
- [ ] Update `CLAUDE.md` "Nix env in worktree subagents" section to note that these tools are now pinned — so subagents can rely on them being present without `apt-get install`
- [ ] One commit with `Bead: vs-2ti` trailer, gitmoji `:wrench:`

## Out of scope
- Do NOT add services-flake or process-compose (that's vs-1bb)
- Do NOT modify flake.nix unless absolutely required (shell.nix edit should suffice)
- Do NOT run `nix build` or heavy evaluations that could take minutes

## Notes for the subagent
- Check nixpkgs package names exact match: `grafana-loki` vs `loki` — on recent nixpkgs it's just `grafana-loki`. Use `nix search nixpkgs <name>` if unsure (may be slow/offline; if so, pick the name that matches the nixpkgs release-25.11 branch — you can WebFetch https://search.nixos.org/packages?channel=25.11&query=<name>).
- The shell.nix uses `pkgs` from a channel-pinned nixpkgs. Trust the channel's package naming.

Adapt lb-skills pipeline (orient, spec, review, test, impl, branch, release,
orchestrator, commit, beads, lint) for SS14/C#/Delta-V development.

## Task
Create .claude/skills/ with all adapted skills. SS14-specific additions:
build, upstream-sync, prototype, changelog.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All lb-skills adapted for C#/.NET/SS14 conventions
- [ ] SS14-specific skills added
- [ ] Skills reference correct tools (dotnet, Content.Tests, YAMLLinter)

title: ops: cross-platform nix dev shell (darwin + windows via wsl2)
description:
## Context
The nix flake (`flake.nix`) currently restricts to `x86_64-linux` + `aarch64-linux`. `shell.nix` pulls libdrm/mesa/xorg/gtk3 — all Linux-only — which causes `nix flake check` to fail on darwin. Windows lacks native nix entirely; any nix-based dev path must go through WSL2.

Goal: every platform gets a usable nix-first dev experience, aligned with the first-class nix stance.

## Task

### macOS (darwin): real flake support
1. Split `shell.nix` into platform-aware structure. Options:
   - (a) `shell.nix` takes a `pkgs` arg, branches on `pkgs.stdenv.isDarwin` / `isLinux`, conditionally includes graphics libs.
   - (b) Factor out `shell/common.nix`, `shell/linux.nix`, `shell/darwin.nix`; `shell.nix` selects based on system.
   Prefer (a) — less file sprawl. The branching is `lib.optionals pkgs.stdenv.isDarwin [...] ++ lib.optionals pkgs.stdenv.isLinux [...]`.
2. Darwin-specific deps: `dotnet-sdk_10` still needed. Skip all X11 / mesa / gtk / libdrm / alsa-lib / wayland — they don't exist on darwin. Graphics on mac goes via Apple frameworks, which Robust/RobustToolbox picks up at runtime if it's built to target darwin. Most of those libs may not be needed at all for a SERVER-ONLY darwin dev shell.
3. Decide scope: **server-only** on darwin (no client deps), or attempt client support. Server-only is much simpler and matches the realistic contributor path (client dev on mac needs a proper Mac build of the engine which is a separate problem). Pick server-only and document that mac contributors who need client rendering should use a Linux VM.
4. Re-enable darwin systems in `flake.nix`: `systems = inputs.flake-parts.lib.systems.flakeExposed;` or the explicit list `[ "x86_64-linux" "aarch64-linux" "x86_64-darwin" "aarch64-darwin" ]`.
5. Verify: `nix flake check --no-build --all-systems` passes (evaluates all four systems; darwin devShell pulls fewer deps).

### Windows: WSL2 documentation path
6. No flake changes for Windows — it has no native nix. Windows contributors install WSL2 with Ubuntu, then clone the repo inside WSL and use the standard Linux nix path.
7. Add a "Windows (via WSL2)" subsection to `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`:
   - Install WSL2: `wsl --install -d Ubuntu-24.04`
   - Inside WSL: install nix (single-user install from https://nixos.org/download), enable flakes, install direnv
   - Clone repo inside WSL filesystem (NOT on Windows drive `/mnt/c/...` — perf disaster)
   - `direnv allow` + standard dev flow
   - Client dev from WSL: GUI apps work via WSLg (Win11+); for audio + OpenGL, this is experimental and may not work reliably. Server-only from WSL is solid.
   - Point macOS contributors who don't want a VM to a similar "server-only nix dev" experience.

### Docs + skill update
8. Update `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`:
   - Quick Start (Nix) section: mention all three platforms (Linux native, macOS native server-only, Windows via WSL2)
   - Add Windows (via WSL2) subsection
   - Note macOS server-only scope
9. Update `.claude/skills/nix/SKILL.md`:
   - Replace the "Flake is Linux-only" warning with an up-to-date matrix:
     | Platform | Native nix | Client shell | Server shell |
     | Linux    | ✅         | ✅           | ✅           |
     | macOS    | ✅         | ❌ (use VM)  | ✅           |
     | Windows  | ❌ use WSL | ❌           | ✅           |
   - Update the "When to use which path" table accordingly
10. Update `CLAUDE.md` Dev Environment section to remove the Linux-only claim and point at `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` for platform specifics.

## Out of scope
- Do not attempt to get the full client stack (graphics, audio) working on darwin or WSLg — deep rabbit hole. Document the limitation and move on.
- Do not modify anything outside `shell.nix`, `flake.nix`, docs, and the /nix skill.
- Do not bump `shell.nix` dependency versions beyond what's needed to get darwin to evaluate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `shell.nix` branches cleanly on `stdenv.isDarwin` / `isLinux`; existing Linux entries unchanged
- [ ] `flake.nix` systems list includes all four (`x86_64-linux`, `aarch64-linux`, `x86_64-darwin`, `aarch64-darwin`)
- [ ] `nix flake check --no-build --all-systems` passes on a Linux host (you may lack a darwin builder — evaluation alone should succeed because flake-parts doesn't require a remote builder for `check`)
- [ ] `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` has a Windows (WSL2) subsection and updated platform framing
- [ ] `.claude/skills/nix/SKILL.md` has an updated platform support matrix
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` reflects cross-platform support
- [ ] Single commit, `Bead: vs-35q` trailer, gitmoji `:sparkles:` or `:construction_worker:`

## Notes
- Windows-via-WSL2 is not really "Windows support" — it's "Linux nix running inside a Windows environment." That's fine and honest; just document clearly.
- Darwin users running this on Apple Silicon should get `aarch64-darwin` automatically.
- If `nix flake check` hits a darwin-specific missing dep we can't resolve, it's OK to conditionally exclude darwin from one specific output (e.g. drop `dev-services` on darwin if services-flake has Linux-only assumptions). Document any such exclusions in the matrix.

title: docs: nix/direnv alternative dev path
description:
## Context
The repo inherits nix flake support from Delta-V:
- .envrc (nix-direnv auto-activation)
- flake.nix / flake.lock (reproducible env)
- shell.nix (40+ deps incl .NET 10, X11, audio, freetype, Wayland, pre-commit)

Decision: keep it as an alternative path but not the canonical one.
Canonical is setup.ubuntu.sh (apt-based).

## Task
Add a brief "Alternative: Nix / direnv" section to docs/DEVELOPMENT.md.
Do NOT bake nix into skills, hooks, or CLAUDE.md — keep it truly optional.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] docs/DEVELOPMENT.md has a Nix section
- [ ] Section documents: `direnv allow` + `nix develop` as alternative setup
- [ ] Notes that this pulls client deps (X11, audio) — use apt --server for server-only hosts
- [ ] Brief note in CLAUDE.md mentioning nix flake is available (one line)
- [ ] No changes to skills, hooks, or setup.ubuntu.sh

title: fix: grafana postgres datasource type mismatch across prod + dev
description:
## Context
vs-2p3 + vs-13x shipped conflicting datasource types for Postgres in Grafana:

- `ops/observability/docker-compose.yml:107` env `GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS: grafana-postgresql-datasource` — installs the plugin
- `ops/observability/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml:29` — provisions datasource with `type: postgres` (built-in)
- `ops/observability/grafana/dashboards/game-servers.json:41` (and panels in perf-metrics.json) — references `"type": "grafana-postgresql-datasource"` (plugin)

When Grafana provisions a datasource with `type: postgres` but a panel asks for `type: grafana-postgresql-datasource`, the panel fails to locate a matching datasource. In the dev services-flake stack the plugin isn't installed at all — no fallback there either.

This will silently break the Connections panel on Game Servers and any postgres-querying panel on Perf Metrics when the operator runs vs-205 verification.

## Task
Pick ONE datasource type and make all three surfaces agree. Recommend `grafana-postgresql-datasource` (the plugin) for parity with upstream SS14 dashboards — postgres-as-plugin offers query builder + TimescaleDB hooks the built-in lacks, and upstream dashboards were authored against it.

### Changes required

1. **`ops/observability/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml`**: change Postgres block `type: postgres` → `type: grafana-postgresql-datasource`. Verify the rest of the block (jsonData, secureJsonData) is still compatible with the plugin schema (it should be — the plugin is a drop-in successor).

2. **`ops/observability/docker-compose.yml`**: confirm `GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS: grafana-postgresql-datasource` is correctly spelled and the plugin installs on grafana 11.3.0 startup. If the plugin requires a specific minimum grafana version, document it. No code change needed unless the image pin is too old.

3. **Dashboards** (`game-servers.json`, `perf-metrics.json`): already reference `grafana-postgresql-datasource` — no change needed. Do grep-verify that NO panel still references `type: postgres` by mistake; if any do, fix.

4. **Dev services-flake stack** (`flake.nix` `perSystem.process-compose."dev-services".services.grafana.graf1`): the plugin isn't in nixpkgs `grafanaPlugins`. Two options:
   - **(a) Install at runtime**: add `GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS=grafana-postgresql-datasource` as an env var / arg to the dev grafana so it fetches the plugin from grafana.com on first start. Services-flake grafana module may expose this via `services.grafana.graf1.settings.plugin_install` or `extraEnv`/`env`. Check the module.
   - **(b) Omit postgres in dev**: drop the Postgres datasource from the dev flake entirely and have contributors use Prometheus + Loki only in dev. Dashboards' postgres panels would show "no datasource" in dev but that's acceptable if documented.

   Prefer **(a)** if the services-flake grafana module supports runtime plugin install; otherwise fall back to **(b)** and document clearly in `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` Dev Services Stack section.

5. **Cross-ref comment sync**: `ops/observability/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml` and the dev flake's inlined grafana datasources should each carry a comment pointing at the other as the "also update me" sibling — reduces drift risk (finding #2 from vs-1bb).

### Validation
- `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml config` parses
- `python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('ops/observability/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml'))"` parses
- Each dashboard JSON still parses
- If option (a) chosen: `nix flake check --no-build --all-systems` passes
- Grep the repo for any remaining `type: postgres` references that should have been updated (and any `grafana-postgresql-datasource` that shouldn't have been touched — the dashboards are correct as-is)

### Commit
One commit, gitmoji `:bug:`, trailer `Bead: vs-3oe`.

## Out of scope
- Do not change image pins unless strictly required by plugin compatibility
- Do not rewrite the dashboard JSONs structurally (they're canonical as vs-13x landed them)
- Do not migrate off `grafana-postgresql-datasource` to another postgres backend

## References
- Plugin page: https://grafana.com/grafana/plugins/grafana-postgresql-datasource/
- services-flake grafana module: https://community.flake.parts/services-flake/services/grafana

title: ops: postgres setup from day 1
description:
## Context
Level 5 hosting uses PostgreSQL instead of the default SQLite. Required for
SS14.Admin (which doesn't support SQLite) and for multi-instance deployments.
We want Postgres from day 1 so DB schema is production-shaped.

SS14 server runs migrations automatically on startup — DB user just needs
to own its database.

## Task
Install PostgreSQL, create vacation_station DB and user, configure SS14 to use
Postgres instead of SQLite, verify migrations run cleanly on first server start.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Installation
- [ ] setup.postgres.sh script that installs postgresql-16 (or 17) via apt
- [ ] systemctl enable postgresql
- [ ] postgresql listening on localhost:5432 (default)

### Database setup
- [ ] DB user `vs14` created with generated password (stored in password manager)
- [ ] Database `vacation_station` created, owned by `vs14`
- [ ] Verified: `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "select 1"`

### SS14 config
- [ ] instances/vacation-station/config.toml includes:
  [database]
  engine = "postgres"
  pg_host = "localhost"
  pg_port = 5432
  pg_database = "vacation_station"
  pg_username = "vs14"
  pg_password = "..."   # from password manager, NOT in repo
- [ ] First server start runs migrations cleanly (check logs: migrations applied N)
- [ ] Verify tables created: `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "\dt"`

### Access control
- [ ] pg_hba.conf restricts connections to localhost (default in most distros)
- [ ] PostgreSQL NOT exposed publicly (port 5432 bound to localhost or firewalled)
- [ ] Password generated with cryptographic entropy (openssl rand, not a dictionary word)

### Backup
- [ ] Cron or systemd timer for `pg_dump -Fc` nightly
- [ ] Backup destination documented (local for now, object storage later)
- [ ] Retention policy documented (e.g. 7 daily, 4 weekly)

### Connection pooling
- [ ] Postgres max_connections reviewed (default 100 usually fine for single instance)
- [ ] No PgBouncer needed initially (revisit if we scale)

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md Postgres section: install, user/DB creation, config wiring,
  backup/restore procedures, migration to S3-compatible storage path
- [ ] Credential rotation procedure documented

title: ops: watchdog setup from day 1
description:
## Context
SS14.Watchdog (codename "Ian") runs the game server as a child process, handles
auto-updates, crash recovery, and exposes an admin API. Required for Level 4+
hosting. We want it from day 1.

Upstream: github.com/space-wizards/SS14.Watchdog
Docs: docs.spacestation14.com/en/server-hosting/setting-up-ss14-watchdog.html

## Dependencies
- Needs .NET 10 SDK + ASP.NET Core 10 Runtime installed (covered by setup.ubuntu.sh --server)

## Task
Build Watchdog from source, deploy as systemd service, configure a vacation-station
instance using the Manifest update provider (or Local initially until we publish
builds).

## Acceptance Criteria

### Installation
- [ ] setup.watchdog.sh script that clones, publishes, and installs to /opt/ss14-watchdog
- [ ] Publish command is exactly: `dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --no-self-contained`
  (variations break Mono.Posix.NETStandard.dll and chmod +x)
- [ ] Binary at /opt/ss14-watchdog/SS14.Watchdog

### systemd unit
- [ ] /etc/systemd/system/ss14-watchdog.service created
- [ ] KillMode=process (required if using PersistServers)
- [ ] OOMPolicy=continue (don't kill watchdog when game server OOMs)
- [ ] Environment="DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/tmp"
- [ ] Restart=on-failure
- [ ] systemctl enable --now ss14-watchdog

### appsettings.yml
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml configured:
  - BaseUrl: http://localhost:5000/
  - Urls: http://*:5000 (or localhost-only + reverse proxy)
  - Serilog with Loki sink pointing at localhost:3100
  - Process.PersistServers: true
  - Notification.DiscordWebhook (optional, for crash alerts)
- [ ] Instance "vacation-station" configured with:
  - ApiToken (generated, NOT in repo — stored in password manager)
  - ApiPort: 1212 (matches game server [status] bind)
  - UpdateType: Local (initially) or Manifest (once we have a CDN)
  - TimeoutSeconds: 60
  - EnvironmentVariables: DOTNET_TieredPGO=1, DOTNET_TC_QuickJitForLoops=1, ROBUST_NUMERICS_AVX=true

### Instance data
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml created
  (this is the GAME SERVER config — hostname, auth mode, metrics, loki, postgres)
- [ ] First build dropped into instances/vacation-station/binaries/ (for Local provider)

### Verification
- [ ] `systemctl is-active ss14-watchdog` returns "active"
- [ ] `curl http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/ping` (or equivalent)
- [ ] Game server starts as child of Watchdog (verify with pstree/ps -ef)
- [ ] Crash recovery: `pkill -f Robust.Server`, confirm Watchdog respawns within timeout
- [ ] API auth test: `curl -u vacation-station:$TOKEN -X POST http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/restart`

### Firewall
- [ ] Port 5000 localhost-only (or add to ufw + cloud firewall if remote control desired)
- [ ] Game server ports (UDP 1212, TCP 1212) open per docs/NETWORKING.md

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md section on Watchdog (install, config, API, troubleshooting)
- [ ] API token storage procedure documented (password manager ref, never commit)
- [ ] Update provider migration path (Local -> Manifest when we have CDN)

title: research: adjacent SS14 services (watchdog, admin, cdn, etc)
human: run vs-3ty postgres setup on host
description:
## Context
vs-3ty shipped the artifacts (setup.postgres.sh, backup units, config template, ops docs) but the orchestrator's worktree subagent is sandboxed and cannot touch the real host. This bead captures the human-only steps to actually bring Postgres up on the production box.

## Task — run on the target host
Execute the scripts from vs-3ty and verify each acceptance criterion. Expect to generate and safely store one credential.

## Steps
1. `sudo ./setup.postgres.sh` (or `PG_PASSWORD=<chosen> sudo -E ./setup.postgres.sh` if you want to bring your own password).
2. Capture the generated password. Store in password manager under "Vacation Station / postgres / vs14". NEVER commit.
3. Verify service: `systemctl is-active postgresql` → "active"; `ss -tln | grep 5432` → listening on 127.0.0.1.
4. Verify DB: `PGPASSWORD=<pw> psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "select 1"` returns 1.
5. Copy `instances/vacation-station/config.toml.example` → `config.toml`, fill in real password. Do NOT commit the filled version (add to `.gitignore` if not already).
6. Confirm pg_hba.conf is localhost-only (Ubuntu default). Confirm port 5432 is NOT exposed publicly (`sudo ufw status` or cloud firewall console).
7. Enable backup timer: `sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable --now ss14-backup.timer`. Confirm `systemctl list-timers | grep ss14-backup` shows next run.
8. Run a manual backup once to prime retention dir: `sudo /opt/vacation-station/ops/postgres/backup.sh`. Confirm output file appears in `/var/backups/vacation-station/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `systemctl is-active postgresql` → active
- [ ] `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station` connects successfully
- [ ] `vs14` password stored in password manager (not in repo, not in shell history)
- [ ] `instances/vacation-station/config.toml` present on host with real password, gitignored
- [ ] Port 5432 not exposed to public internet (firewall-verified)
- [ ] `ss14-backup.timer` enabled + active; at least one backup file exists
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp in this bead before closing

## Notes
Report any deviations from setup.postgres.sh (e.g. postgresql-17 instead of 16, different install layout) so we can PR fixes back into the script.
